About Privacy policy Support

Privacy Policy for Dojo: Master Meditation

Last updated: February 17, 2026

This Privacy Policy describes how Medidojo Inc. (“Company,” “we,” “us,” or “our”) collects, uses, and discloses information when you use the Dojo: Master Meditation mobile application and the website located at https://medidojo.com (collectively, the “Service”).

By using the Service, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

1. Definitions

Account — A unique account created for you to access our Service.

Company — Medidojo Inc., a company based in California, United States.

Application — Dojo: Master Meditation, the mobile software application provided by the Company.

Device — Any device capable of accessing the Service, including a smartphone, tablet, or computer.

Personal Data — Information that identifies or can be used to identify an individual.

Usage Data — Information collected automatically through use of the Service.

Service Providers — Third-party companies or individuals who process data on our behalf to support the Service.

2. Information We Collect

Personal Data

We may collect the following information:

  • Email address
  • Account login information
  • Information you provide when contacting us
  • Payment and subscription status (processed via third-party providers)

We do not store full credit card information. Payments are processed securely through app stores or third-party payment processors.

Usage Data

We automatically collect certain technical data, including:

  • IP address
  • Device type and operating system
  • App version
  • Pages or screens viewed
  • Time spent in the app
  • Diagnostic and crash data

When accessed via mobile device, we may collect:

  • Mobile device unique ID
  • Mobile operating system
  • Device identifiers
  • Interaction and usage analytics

3. Health and Biometric Data

If you choose to connect wearable devices or Apple Health, the Service may access heart rate and related physiological data to personalize meditation sessions and improve your experience.

This data:

  • Is accessed only with your permission
  • Is used solely to provide app functionality
  • Is not sold to third parties
  • Is not used for advertising

You can revoke health data permissions at any time through your device settings.

4. How We Use Your Information

We use Personal Data to:

  • Provide and maintain the Service
  • Manage user accounts
  • Personalize meditation sessions
  • Process subscriptions
  • Improve product performance and user experience
  • Analyze usage trends
  • Communicate updates, security notices, or support responses
  • Comply with legal obligations

We may also use aggregated, anonymized data for analytics and product improvement.

5. Sharing of Information

We may share information:

  • With Service Providers — Such as hosting providers, analytics platforms, authentication providers, and subscription processors.
  • With Business Partners — Only when necessary to provide features or promotions.
  • For Legal Compliance — If required by law, court order, or government request.
  • In Business Transfers — In connection with a merger, acquisition, or asset sale.

We do not sell your Personal Data.

6. Data Retention

We retain Personal Data only as long as necessary to:

  • Provide the Service
  • Comply with legal obligations
  • Resolve disputes
  • Enforce agreements

Usage Data may be retained for internal analytics and security purposes.

7. Data Security

We implement reasonable administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect your data.

No system is completely secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security.

8. Your Rights

Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:

  • Access your Personal Data
  • Correct inaccurate data
  • Request deletion
  • Restrict or object to processing
  • Request data portability

You may request deletion by contacting us or using in-app account settings if available.

California residents may have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA).

9. Children’s Privacy

The Service is not intended for children under 13.

We do not knowingly collect Personal Data from children under 13. If we become aware that such data has been collected, we will delete it promptly.

10. International Transfers

Your data may be processed in the United States or other jurisdictions where our Service Providers operate.

By using the Service, you consent to such transfers.

11. Third-Party Links

Our Service may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.

12. Changes to This Privacy Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time.

We will update the “Last updated” date at the top of this page. Continued use of the Service after changes means you accept the updated policy.

13. Contact Us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact us at:

Email: asaf@medidojo.com

Medidojo Inc.
California, United States

© 2026 Medidojo Inc. All rights reserved.
